source_pr.title: test: fix `TestAggToCopHint`
source_pr.body:
<!--
Thank you for contributing to TiDB! Please read TiDB's [CONTRIBUTING](https://github.com/pingcap/community/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) document **BEFORE** filing this PR.
-->

### What problem does this PR solve? <!--add issue link with summary if exists-->
fix #14472 

### What is changed and how it works?
Because the test use `mysql.opt_rule_blacklist` to avoid eliminating aggregation by primary key. But some other cases change the global `opt_rule_blacklist` table too. We can just create a new table without the primary key to make the test stable.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

 - Unit test
 - Integration test

symptoms: PR body says other cases change global mysql.opt_rule_blacklist too; "create a new table without the primary key to make the test stable".
failure_signature: PR body says other cases change global mysql.opt_rule_blacklist too; "create a new table without the primary key to make the test stable".
root_cause_explanation: The test relied on the shared system table `mysql.opt_rule_blacklist` to control optimizer rules, but other tests could modify the same table and cause intermittent failures. The PR stabilizes the test by removing the dependency on `mysql.opt_rule_blacklist` and using a dedicated local table setup for the assertion.
fix_pattern: Avoid using the shared `mysql.opt_rule_blacklist` table; create a local table in the test to remove cross-test interference.

evidence: evidence: PR body says other cases change global mysql.opt_rule_blacklist too; "create a new table without the primary key to make the test stable".
evidence: diff removes mysql.opt_rule_blacklist dependency and uses a local table ta (tk.MustExec("create table ta(a int, b int, index(a))")) in TestAggToCopHint (planner/core/physical_plan_test.go).
